description: Evaluates the broker-comm skill on synthetic deal and communication scenarios to test draft quality, reply detection logic, follow-up timing, satisfaction assessment, and pass sequencing.

evals.name: Draft initial outreach — SHORTLISTED deal, broker not yet contacted
evals.prompt:
Draft an outreach email for this deal:

**Deal Name:** Triangle Sparkle Janitorial
**Deal ID:** DEAL-004 (internal only)
**State:** SHORTLISTED
**Broker:** Mark Hendricks, Southeast Business Sales
**Broker Email:** mhendricks@sebiz.com
**Listing Source:** BizBuySell
**Business Type:** Commercial janitorial cleaning
**Location:** Raleigh, NC
**Asking Price:** $1,200,000
**Revenue:** $820,000
**SDE:** $310,000
**SDE Multiple:** 3.87x
**Key Listing Details:** 12-year-old business, 47 commercial contracts, absentee-operated per broker, 8 employees
**Broker Vetting:** Mark Hendricks / Southeast Business Sales was vetted on 2026-03-15. Score: 16/20 (Tier 1). PROCEED verdict.
**Communications Array:** [] (empty — no prior contact)

Draft the initial outreach email. Do not send it.

assertions.text: Output shows full draft email text (subject line + body) before any action is taken
Email is NOT sent and NOT saved as a Gmail draft without explicit user approval — skill asks for confirmation
NDA is requested in the email body
Draft references specific deal details: business type (janitorial), asking price ($1,200,000), location (Raleigh NC), or the 12-year tenure — NOT generic boilerplate
Internal deal ID (DEAL-004) does NOT appear anywhere in the draft email text
No mention of visa status, H1B, immigration, or work authorization in the draft
Output notes that deal state would move to CONTACTING once the email is sent (not when drafted)

evals.name: Check replies — 2 CONTACTING deals, 1 has reply, 1 silent 8 days
evals.prompt:
Check for broker replies on all active deals.

**Active Deals in CONTACTING state:**

Deal A: Peach State Washers (laundromat, Atlanta, GA)
- Broker: Sandra Lee, slee@atlantabiz.com
- Initial email sent: 2026-03-28 (8 days ago from today 2026-04-05)
- gmail_thread_id: thread_abc123
- No follow-up sent yet

Deal B: Carolina Suds Laundry (laundromat, Charlotte, NC)
- Broker: David Park, dpark@carolinabiz.com
- Initial email sent: 2026-04-01 (4 days ago from today 2026-04-05)
- gmail_thread_id: thread_def456

**Gmail search results (simulated):**
- thread_abc123: 2 messages — 1 outbound from buyer (2026-03-28), 1 inbound from Sandra Lee (2026-04-03) with subject 'Re: Acquisition Inquiry — Laundromat, Atlanta GA' — body: 'Hi, thanks for reaching out. NDA attached. Please sign and return and I'll get you the CIM.'
- thread_def456: 1 message — 1 outbound from buyer (2026-04-01). No reply.

Summarize the reply status for all CONTACTING deals.

assertions.text: Output shows a summary table or structured list covering both deals with reply status, days waiting, and broker names
Peach State Washers (Deal A) is correctly identified as REPLIED with the reply date 2026-04-03 noted
Carolina Suds Laundry (Deal B) is correctly identified as SILENT with 4 days of silence noted
Deal A reply content is summarized: NDA was attached, CIM is pending NDA signature
Deal B (silent 4 days) is NOT flagged at the 14-day threshold level — it may be noted as within normal range
Output suggests next action for Deal A: user should sign NDA to receive CIM (or similar actionable next step)
Output calls gmail_search_messages or gmail_read_thread to check for replies — does not assume from memory

evals.name: Follow-up nudge — deal CONTACTING for 12 days with no reply
evals.prompt:
Draft a follow-up email for this deal.

**Deal Name:** Gulf Coast Clean Pro (janitorial, Tampa, FL)
**State:** CONTACTING
**Broker:** James Whitfield, jwhitfield@gulfbiz.com
**Initial email sent:** 2026-03-24 (12 days ago from today 2026-04-05)
**Communications array:** [
  {
    'date': '2026-03-24',
    'direction': 'outbound',
    'type': 'inquiry',
    'subject': 'Acquisition Inquiry — Commercial Janitorial, Tampa FL',
    'summary': 'Initial outreach, NDA requested, asked 3 deal-specific questions.',
    'status': 'sent',
    'gmail_thread_id': 'thread_ghi789'
  }
]
**No inbound messages from broker.**

Draft a follow-up nudge. Do not send it.

assertions.text: Output shows full draft email text (subject line + body) before any action
Email is NOT sent and NOT saved as Gmail draft without explicit user approval
Draft references the number of days since first contact (12 days) explicitly, or references the original email date
Tone is professional and brief — does not contain pressure language, ultimatums, or expressions of frustration
Draft is under 100 words (Follow-Up Nudge protocol requirement)
Subject line uses 'Re:' prefix with the original subject line to keep the email in-thread
Output does NOT trigger the 14-day CAUTION block because 12 days is below the 14-day escalation threshold (or if it does, threshold is correctly identified as 14 days)

evals.name: Satisfaction check — ready to meet, all critical questions answered
evals.prompt:
Run a satisfaction check on this deal.

**Deal Name:** Blue Ridge Shine (car wash, Asheville, NC)
**Business Type:** Tunnel car wash
**State:** IN_DILIGENCE
**Broker:** Cathy Nguyen, cnguyen@mountainbiz.com
**gmail_thread_id:** thread_jkl012

**Thread summary (simulated gmail_read_thread result):**
- Round 1 questions sent 2026-03-10
- Broker replied 2026-03-13:
  * Confirmed TTM revenue $1.1M and SDE $420K with add-back detail (owner salary $95K, personal auto $18K, one-time equipment repair $22K)
  * 3-year P&L and tax returns sent as attachments
  * Confirmed manager (Marcus, 4 years tenure) in place and willing to stay
  * Owner works approx 5 hours/week, mostly reviews reports
  * Lease: 7 years remaining, $8,200/month base rent, option for 2x5yr renewal, assignment clause confirmed
  * 1,240 active monthly unlimited memberships at $35/month avg
  * Annual churn rate ~22% on memberships
  * No pending lawsuits, all permits current and transferable
  * Water reclaim system in place, no open EPA issues

**Open items NOT yet addressed:**
- Top 5 customers / concentration (N/A for car wash — membership model, no single large customer)
- Equipment age and maintenance records (not yet asked)

Is this deal ready for a seller intro call?

assertions.text: Verdict is READY_TO_MEET
Output shows a structured table or list mapping which diligence categories have been asked and answered
Confirmed items are explicitly listed: SDE verification, add-backs, manager in place, lease terms, membership count, no legal issues
Equipment age gap is noted but correctly classified as non-blocking for READY_TO_MEET (minor gap addressable in the meeting)
Output offers to draft a Serious Interest Letter (Template 4) or request for seller meeting as the recommended next action
Output uses gmail_read_thread to retrieve thread context — does not rely on memory alone

evals.name: Satisfaction check — NOT ready, two critical gaps remain
evals.prompt:
Run a satisfaction check on this deal.

**Deal Name:** Sunshine Commercial Cleaning (janitorial, Orlando, FL)
**Business Type:** Commercial janitorial
**State:** IN_DILIGENCE
**Broker:** Tony Ramirez, tramirez@sunshinebiz.com
**gmail_thread_id:** thread_mno345

**Thread summary (simulated gmail_read_thread result):**
- Round 1 questions sent 2026-03-15
- Broker replied 2026-03-18:
  * TTM revenue $730K confirmed
  * SDE stated as $245K — add-backs mentioned as 'owner salary and some personal expenses' but no line-item detail provided
  * Confirmed 6 full-time employees and 1 part-time supervisor
  * Owner works 20-25 hours/week, handles all client relationships personally
  * Lease: confirmed $4,500/month rent but expiration date 'will be confirmed' — never followed up
  * No lawsuits confirmed

**Open items:**
- SDE add-back detail: stated but not itemized (critical gap)
- Customer concentration: never asked or answered — top 5 customers + percentages unknown
- Lease expiry and assignment clause: broker promised to confirm but never did
- Owner works 20-25 hrs/week and handles all client relationships personally — major absentee concern flagged

Is this deal ready for a seller intro call?

assertions.text: Verdict is NOT_READY
All unresolved gaps are explicitly listed: SDE add-backs not itemized, customer concentration unknown, lease expiry unconfirmed
Owner dependence (20-25 hrs/week, personal client relationships) is flagged as a critical concern for an absentee buyer
Output offers to draft a targeted follow-up email addressing specifically the identified gaps (not a generic follow-up)
The draft follow-up email (if shown) pulls specific questions from diligence_question_bank.md categories: F3 (add-back itemization), C1 (customer concentration), L1 (lease expiry and assignment)
Output does NOT recommend moving to READY_TO_MEET or suggest a seller call

evals.name: Soft pass — user decides to pass on deal in CONTACTING state
evals.prompt:
I want to pass on this deal. Draft the appropriate email to the broker.

**Deal Name:** Magnolia Washout (car wash, Birmingham, AL)
**Deal ID:** DEAL-007 (internal only)
**State:** CONTACTING
**Broker:** Patricia Wells, pwells@magnoliabrokers.com
**Initial email sent:** 2026-03-20
**Broker replied:** 2026-03-22 with NDA — buyer has not yet signed
**Reason for passing:** After reviewing the financials more carefully, the SDE multiple at asking price is 4.9x — too rich given equipment age. Not worth pursuing further.

Draft a soft pass email. The broker has been professional and responsive. Keep the door open.

assertions.text: Output shows full draft email text (subject line + body) before any action
Email is NOT sent and NOT saved as Gmail draft without explicit user approval — skill asks for confirmation
Tone is professional and appreciative — does not contain criticism of the business, the price, or the broker
Email keeps the door open for future deals — contains language inviting the broker to share future listings in the target vertical/region
The reason for passing ('SDE multiple too high', '4.9x', or the specific valuation rationale) does NOT appear in the draft email text sent to the broker
Internal deal ID (DEAL-007) does NOT appear in the draft email text
Output notes that deal state would move to PASSED pending user approval to send the email
